items.title: The National | Pilots killed in Air Canada runway crash
items.summary_auto: March 23, 2026 | Two pilots were killed and several passengers were injured when an Air Canada Express plane crashed into a fire truck at New York’s LaGuardia airport. Trump’s optimism about war in the Middle East is reflected in the stock market, but rejected by Iran. And, posters tease Céline Dion’s rumoured return to the stage in Paris.

items.title: N.S. judge to rule on proposed $18M settlement in 2015 Air Canada Halifax crash landing
items.summary_auto: The class action was filed in 2015 and scheduled for trial earlier this year, but a notice released Monday said the proposed settlement could avoid a lengthy court process and probably offer larger compensation.

items.title: Jury finds Bill Cosby sexually assaulted woman in 1972, awards her more than $19M US
items.summary_auto: A civil jury in California found Monday that Bill Cosby was liable for drugging and sexually assaulting a woman in 1972 and awarded her $19.25 million US.

items.title: The moment a plane smashed into a fire truck at LaGuardia Airport | About That
items.summary_auto: Air Canada Flight 8646 collided with a fire truck at New York's LaGuardia Airport, killing two pilots and injuring dozens of others. Andrew Chang breaks down what we know about the crash from air traffic control audio and insight from aviation experts. Images provided by The Canadian Press, Reuters and Getty Images

items.title: How Major League Baseball players can turn to a robot to challenge balls, strikes this year
items.summary_auto: Here's a brief explanation of how Major League Baseball will use technology this year to give players the opportunity to challenge calls on balls and strikes at the plate.

items.title: 4 people dead in 2 separate northwest B.C. avalanches Sunday
items.summary_auto: RCMP say three heli-skiers were killed in an avalanche on Mount Knauss just north of Terrace, B.C., in the province's northwest on Sunday. The same day, one person was killed in a separate avalanche, near the B.C.-Alaska border.

items.title: Bodycam video shows Justin Timberlake's 2024 impaired driving arrest
items.summary_auto: Justin Timberlake struggled to perform field sobriety tests requiring him to walk a straight line and stand on one leg after he was pulled over in New York's Hamptons in 2024 by police officers who suspected him of driving drunk, according to video footage released Friday.

items.title: Footage of Justin Timberlake's 2024 impaired driving arrest released
items.summary_auto: Justin Timberlake struggled to perform field sobriety tests requiring him to walk a straight line and stand on one leg after he was pulled over in New York's Hamptons in 2024 by police officers who suspected him of driving drunk, according to video footage released Friday.

items.title: One dead following avalanche near B.C.-Alaska border
items.summary_auto: B.C. RCMP say police got a satellite SOS Sunday afternoon reporting an avalanche. It happened near Pleasant Camp and the Klehini River, close to the B.C.-Alaska border.

items.title: The facts about U.S. air traffic controllers
items.summary_auto: Air traffic controllers in the U.S. are understaffed and overworked. Here are the facts about the job.

items.title: British police investigate burning of 4 ambulances belonging to Jewish charity
items.summary_auto: Four ambulances belonging to a Jewish charity were set on fire early Monday in London in what British police are investigating as an antisemitic hate crime. Detectives are working to determine whether a claim of responsibility from a group with alleged links to Iran is authentic.

items.title: Quebec resident among 2 pilots killed in crash at New York's LaGuardia airport
items.summary_auto: Quebec resident Antoine Forest was one of the pilots killed in the Sunday night crash at New York's LaGuardia airport, according to Radio-Canada sources.

items.title: Canadian military members safe after reports of damage at UN peacekeeping camp
items.summary_auto: Canadian Armed Forces members deployed in the Middle East are safe after a missile was intercepted on Sunday near the Israel-Lebanon border, the defence minister's office says.

items.title: Victoria Mboko advances to Miami Open quarterfinals, defeating doubles partner Mirra Andreeva
items.summary_auto: Canada's Victoria Mboko earned a 7-6 (4), 4-6, 6-0 fourth-round win over Russia's Mirra Andreeva on Monday at the Miami Open tennis tournament.

items.title: Liberals' fiscal watchdog nominee vows to hold government's 'feet to the fire'
items.summary_auto: The Liberal government's nominee to be the next fiscal watchdog insists she'll run an independent and non-partisan ship — even though her candidacy has created a political rift.

items.title: Federal ICE agents patrolling some U.S. airports amid security staff shortages
items.summary_auto: Armed U.S. federal immigration officers in tactical gear moved through terminals at some of the busiest U.S. airports Monday, standing near security lines and checkpoints after U.S. President Donald Trump ordered their deployment during a partial government shutdown that has disrupted air travel nationwide.

items.title: Ottawa asks Federation of Sovereign Indigenous Nations to pay back $28.7 million after forensic audit
items.summary_auto: The federal government is asking the Federation of Sovereign Indigenous Nations to repay more than $28.7 million following a forensic audit into its spending.

items.title: Turns out these sharks can make friends and have social lives, new study finds
items.summary_auto: New research is suggesting that not all sharks are the lone, cold, ruthless predators as many films so often depict them. Some species, it turns out, can even be friendly — at least among their own kind.

items.title: Death toll rises to at least 33 in Colombian military plane crash
items.summary_auto: A Colombian military transport plane with 121 people on board, mostly soldiers, crashed shortly after taking off Monday in Puerto Leguizamo, Colombia, killing at least 33 people and leaving at least 81 injured, according to the town's deputy mayor.

items.title: How war in the Middle East is upending the global economy
items.summary_auto: Stuck ships, wrong ports, and higher costs: war in the Middle East is causing headaches for worldwide supply chains. New York Times reporter Peter S. Goodman explores the mounting impacts on supply chains that remain highly integrated.

items.title: When it comes to sports betting, does everyone lose?
items.summary_auto: With the legalization of sports betting in Canada, and much of the United States, watching your favourite team has changed. If you don't partake, you're bombarded with ads, and if you do, it's likely changed the way you view and cheer on your team. But it's changed the game for players, officials, and sports journalists too. Author Danny Funt on his new book Everyone Loses, The Tumultuous Rise of American Sports Gambling.

items.title: Quebec's housing tribunal rules no-pet clause in rental lease violates Charter rights
items.summary_auto: A recent ruling by Quebec’s housing tribunal has found the no-pet clause of a rental lease to be in violation of the province's Charter of Human Rights and Freedoms.

items.title: Kendra Duggar, wife of 19 Kids and Counting's Joseph Duggar, arrested
items.summary_auto: Two days after the arrest of former 19 Kids and Counting star Joseph Duggar, an arrest warrant for his wife Kendra Duggar was also issued and served.

items.title: Arborist rescues cat from 8-storey-tall tree in east Ottawa
items.summary_auto: Arborist Tyler Sirrs saw social media posts about a cat stuck for days in a tall tree in Orléans and decided to help. He described the rescue to CBC Ottawa Morning host Rebecca Zandbergen.

items.title: WNBA players vote to ratify 7-year collective bargaining agreement through 2032
items.summary_auto: WNBA players unanimously approved the new collective bargaining agreement on Monday with more than 90 per cent participating in voting over the weekend.

items.title: N.L. 'prolific offender' arrested again, 2 weeks after judge granted ‘last chance’ bail
items.summary_auto: A 34-year-old Conception Bay North man dubbed a “prolific offender” by the RCMP was arrested again on Sunday evening, less than two weeks after being granted bail.

items.title: Trial stayed for Manitoba man accused of killing mother as teen
items.summary_auto: The family of a man accused of killing his mother when he was a teen applauded and cried in a Winnipeg courtroom after a Manitoba judge ordered a stay of proceedings in the case Monday morning.

items.title: RCMP failed to recruit enough officers to meet operational needs: auditor general
items.summary_auto: The RCMP has not recruited enough new officers or effectively assigned its members to meet its operational needs — raising concerns about public safety across the country, according to a new report from Canada's auditor general.

items.title: Ontario couple in murder trial had no 'calculated scheme' to harm boys in their care, defence argues
Up to 40 cm of snow, strong winds expected to batter southeast Newfoundland
items.summary_auto: While spring technically started late last week, Newfoundland and Labrador isn't free from winter weather just yet.

items.title: Oil prices fall after Trump postpones Iran strikes
items.summary_auto: Oil prices fell on Monday morning after President Donald Trump said the U.S. was holding off on striking Iran’s energy infrastructure amid productive talks between the two countries.

items.title: Cubans turn to faith and family amid nationwide blackout
items.summary_auto: Cuban authorities said late Sunday that the electrical system in Havana, which is regularly rationed between neighbourhoods, had returned to normal operation.

items.title: Lawyers challenging Quebec's Bill 21 tell Supreme Court it violates Constitution, sets dangerous precedent
Why First Nations in B.C. are buying up casinos in the age of online gambling
items.summary_auto: Both the CEO of a First Nations development arm and an academic say acquiring these businesses provides economic incentive at a time when nations still face barriers to generating wealth.

items.title: Trump adds new statue of Christopher Columbus near White House
items.summary_auto: Trump endorses a traditional view of Columbus as a leader of the 1492 mission seen as the unofficial beginning of European colonization in the Americas and the development of the modern economic and political order.

items.title: Sask. is a potash and agriculture giant. So why is conflict in the Middle East a problem for farmers?
items.summary_auto: In 2024, nearly a third of global potash production came from Canada, according to Natural Resources Canada .

items.title: Canada-wide arrest warrant issued following N.L. truck crash that left site looking like a 'war zone'
items.summary_auto: More than a year after a transport truck went over an embankment in Newfoundland’s Burin Peninsula, the Crown says the company’s president is in India and out of reach.

items.title: Trump extends deadline for Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz
items.summary_auto: U.S. President Donald Trump said Monday the U.S. was talking with a "respected" Iranian leader and claimed the Islamic Republic was eager for a deal to end the war. He also extended a deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz or face attacks on its power plants, saying it has an additional five days.

items.title: U.S. officials say both black boxes recovered from fatal Air Canada runway crash
Maple syrup season in N.S. off to good start, despite concerns about last year's drought
items.summary_auto: Some maple producers had been worried that the dry conditions last year would affect sap runs this year, but it's business as usual so far, producers say.

items.title: Doug Ford prepares to unveil 8th Ontario budget amid economic uncertainty
items.summary_auto: Politicians return to Queen’s Park Monday for a shortened spring session, as Premier Doug Ford’s government is set to unveil its eight budget in just days. The legislature returns a month later than usual after the Progressive Conservatives delayed the resumption of the session.

items.title: Australia is settling into age-restricted social media. Canada is mulling whether to join in
items.summary_auto: Nearly four months into Australia's landmark social media ban for users under 16, parent proponents laud it as a valiant first step towards a safer online experience for youth. Yet as other countries weigh introducing their own social age minimum, some continue to question bans as a solution.

items.title: Dual citizens weigh Trump, taxes in decision to renounce U.S. citizenship
items.summary_auto: Every year, thousands of dual citizens living abroad choose to formally renounce their U.S. citizenship. They say Trump is a major factor in their decision — but not the only one.
